title: Norway Coordinated Register Notification (Samordnet registermelding, BR-1010BM)
description: Brønnøysundregistrene's (the Brønnøysund Register Centre) main coordinated register notification form, used to register a business or company in the Central Coordinating Register for Legal Entities (Enhetsregisteret) and, at the same time, in the Register of Business Enterprises (Foretaksregisteret), NAV's Employer/Employee Register (Aa-registeret), Statistics Norway's Register of Business and Enterprises, the Register of Foundations (Stiftelsesregisteret), and the Directorate of Taxes' register of non-personal taxpayers, across many legal entity types (sole proprietorship, general/limited partnership, private/public limited company, foreign branch (NUF), foundation, cooperative, and others). This document models the form's core, entity-type-independent identity/address/contact/registration sections in full, together with the governance sections (board and participants, signature and procuration arrangements, auditor, accountant, corporate group, and final signatures) that apply broadly across most registered legal entity types. It deliberately does not model every entity-type-specific or event-specific sub-section the coordinated form also contains (articles of association/partnership agreement; share/partnership capital; capital reduction; merger; demerger; and the separate Norway address block for a foreign-registered enterprise) — see this document's own VERIFICATION.md for the disclosed, out-of-scope companion-schedule candidates this represents. This document describes the form only; it does not submit anything, and does not imply Norwegian government endorsement.

source.documentRef: BR-1010BM, "Samordnet registermelding" ("Coordinated register notification"), Hovedblankett Enhetsregisteret, edition "BR – 1010B - 2026" — Brønnøysundregistrene's own fillable AcroForm PDF

verification.notes: GOV-2316 "GovSchema Standard Research" cycle. Opens Norway as the registry's 35th jurisdiction, via Business Formation. Norway had been named across at least two prior Nordic scouting passes (GOV-2276, GOV-2292) as a parallel-scout candidate; this cycle picked up Brønnøysundregistrene's own central multi-purpose registration form directly. Fetched fresh from brreg.no with a plain HTTP GET: HTTP 200, 2,313,349 bytes -- matching this issue's own pre-recorded figure exactly -- genuine %PDF-1.6, 6 pages, no login/CAPTCHA/WAF gate. A pdfjs-dist extraction (per-page getAnnotations({intent:'display'}) plus a separate doc.getFieldObjects() cross-check) resolved 380 raw /Widget annotations (207 Tx, 173 Btn, 0 Ch) across 6 pages and 386 distinct names via getFieldObjects() -- matching this issue's own pre-recorded figures exactly. The 386-vs-380 gap reconciles exactly: 7 of the 386 getFieldObjects() names are non-terminal radio-group parent names whose child widgets are separately named (e.g. "Check Box70" parents kids "Check Box70.0"/".1"/".4"), each already counted individually among the 380 widgets; the remaining 379 distinct widget-linked names include exactly one non-unique pair -- "Postnummer7" is assigned, apparently by PDF-authoring error, to two different widgets on the same row (a narrow ~47pt box and a wide ~213pt box), disambiguated here by widget geometry rather than name (see transferToPostalCode/transferToTown below). A disposable reconciliation script (build-schema.mjs plus reconcile.mjs, not committed) asserts, and confirms, that every one of the 380 widgets maps to exactly one of: a fields[] entry (318 widgets, consolidating to 191 fields[] entries via 1 legal-entity-type 26-checkbox-to-enum consolidation, 1 notification-type 4-checkbox-to-enum consolidation, 2 register/employer Yes/No pairs, a signature-arrangement 11-checkbox-to-enum consolidation, a procuration 2-checkbox-to-enum consolidation, an 11-slot bounded board/participants table each with a 7-checkbox role group consolidated to one enum per slot, a 2-slot contact-person table each with its own role-checkbox consolidation, several split date/year widget merges, and the disclosed Postnummer7 name-collision split); a disclosed out-of-scope companion-schedule section (61 widgets across sections 14/15/16/17/18/24, see Scope below); or a confirmed non-rendering artifact (1 widget, "Check Box84", zero-height rect [499,170,514,170], excluded as it renders no visible checkbox on the page). 380 = 318 + 61 + 1, 0 unmapped, 0 double-mapped. This document has 0 documents[] entries: unlike this registry's fi/prh precedent, this specimen has no fillable enclosure-checklist widget group naming specific appendix forms to attach; the form's own footer note ("Husk de nødvendige vedleggene. Se veiledningen for oversikt.") points to external, non-form-embedded guidance instead -- a disclosed structural fact, not an oversight, matching this registry's fi/vero 50a precedent's own zero-documents rationale. Scope: this coordinated form is BRREG's single shared intake for many legal entity types (sole proprietorship, general/limited partnership, private/public limited company, foreign branch (NUF), foundation, cooperative, savings bank, and 18 further named types) at once. Per this cycle's own brief, this schema models the entity-type-independent core (name/organisation number, notification type, other-register registration, four address blocks, submitter, the 26-value legal-entity-type selector itself, establishment/sub-unit-transfer dates, activity description, two contact-person slots, written-language preference) plus the governance sections that apply broadly across most registered entity types, not just one (11-slot board/participants table, signature-right arrangement, procuration arrangement, auditor, accountant, corporate-group flags, free-text remarks, notification addresses, and the final 12-slot signatory block) -- in total 191 fields[]. It explicitly does NOT model six entity-type- or event-specific sub-sections, disclosed here as deferred companion-schedule candidates for a future cycle rather than silently dropped: section 14 (Vedtekter/selskapsavtale -- articles of association/partnership agreement, 2 widgets, relevant only once a company/foundation is registered in the Register of Business Enterprises and/or Register of Foundations); section 15 (Kapital -- share/partnership capital, 7 widgets, AS/ASA/KS/foundation-specific); section 16 (Vedtak om kapitalnedsettelse -- capital reduction decision, 15 widgets, AS/ASA-specific); section 17 (Fusjon -- merger, 11 widgets, event-specific); section 18 (Fisjon -- demerger, 16 widgets, event-specific); and section 24 (Navn og adresse med mer for virksomheten i Norge, 10 widgets, applies only to a Norwegian-registered foreign enterprise, NUF). Together these 6 sections account for 61 of the specimen's 380 widgets (16%). The form's own page-3 instruction states plainly that a sole proprietorship (Enkeltpersonforetak) skips fields 14-20 entirely ("Enkeltpersonforetak skal ikke fylle ut feltene 14-20"), underscoring that these are genuinely entity-type-gated, not universal, sections. Judgment calls and disclosed quirks: (1) the 26-value legalEntityType enum's own printed grid places explanatory "må spesifiseres i felt 26" (must be specified in field 26) notes beside four of its values (Offentlig sektor, Europeisk foretak/selskap, Annen juridisk person, and -- per the specimen's own inconsistent formatting -- ambiguously for Pensjonskasser, whose own note text could not be confirmed at its exact printed position during this cycle's x-coordinate-based text-extraction pass); field 26 (Andre merknader/opplysninger) is in scope as otherRemarks, so this dependency is satisfiable but not mechanically enforced via requiredWhen, since GovSchema v0.3 has no direct way to require one field's content conditional on another field's enum value without inventing a synthetic boolean the source does not print. (2) The Postnummer7 shared-name pair (see above) is modelled as two distinct fields (transferToPostalCode/transferToTown) by widget geometry, with the naming defect disclosed in each field's own description rather than silently merged into one field or silently treated as if uniquely named. (3) The 11-row board/participants table (section 19) is one row larger than this cycle's own initial pass assumed (10, based on the "Ansvarsdel"/"Spesielle opplysninger" numbering visible up to suffix 10) -- an 11th row (Fødselsnummer23/Navn23/Adresse23/Postnr23/Poststed23 plus a separately-numbered Ansvarsdel11/Spesielle opplysninger11 pair) was caught only by this cycle's own disposable reconciliation script reporting 2 unmapped widgets on a first pass; corrected before finalizing, and flagged here as an illustration of why the reconciliation script's own zero-unmapped assertion, not a manual widget count, is the source of truth. (4) headOfficeMobileNumber's underlying widget ("Text14") carries no alternativeText tooltip and a generic AcroForm name, unlike its neighbours; its identity as the mobile-number field is inferred from its x-coordinate position beneath the printed column header "Mobiltelefonnummer Telefonnummer Hjemmeside" (confirmed via a dedicated per-line, per-item x-coordinate extraction pass after this cycle's first, coarser y-only line-clustering pass merged the three header words into a single ambiguous string) -- disclosed as a labelling gap in the source, not a structural fact silently assumed. (5) The 7-role board-member checkbox group and 5-role (2-slot contact-person) checkbox groups are each modelled as one enum per slot rather than independent booleans, per this registry's radio-group-as-booleans precedent, since the source's own "Kryss av for riktig rolle" (tick the correct role) instruction and single-checkbox-per-person layout both indicate single-selection, not multi-select. (6) The second contact-person slot's own role-checkbox group has 4 options, not 5 -- no "Innehaver" (owner) column exists in that row's checkboxes on the specimen itself, consistent with sole ownership being nameable only once; disclosed in contactPerson2Role's own description. (7) Employee board representation (marking a board member with \"A\" for \"elected by the employees\") has no corresponding fillable widget on this specimen -- it is a hand-annotation convention described in the form's own instructions, not modelled as schema-level structure, and is disclosed in boardMemberNRole's own description rather than silently ignored. (8) The auditor/accountant sections' own printed \"Jeg bekrefter ... (underskrift)\" (I confirm ... (signature)) lines require an ink signature with no corresponding fillable widget on this specimen (like this registry's fi/prh signatureAndPrintedName precedent) -- not modelled as a separate field, since there is no widget to represent. Mock conformance run: two scenarios were built and checked against this schema's own required/requiredWhen/crossFieldValidation grammar with a from-scratch interpreter script (conformance-check.mjs, not committed): (1) minimal-sole-proprietorship-new-registration.json -- a sole proprietorship's first-time registration, exercising only the statically-required fields (legalName, notificationType, headOfficeStreetAddress/PostalCode/Town, activityDescription, signatureDate, signatoryPrintedName1) plus legalEntityType and the notification-address pair, both conditionally required by notificationType=new_business. 0 errors. (2) private-limited-company-full-governance.json -- a private limited company (AS) change-of-registration notification exercising organisationNumber (required for a non-new-business notification), a 3-member board with distinct roles, a signature arrangement, an auditor, an accountant, and the corporate-group flags. 0 errors. 4 independent mutation controls, each expected to raise exactly 1 error, all confirmed: dropping legalName (statically required) -> 1 error; setting notificationType=new_business while omitting legalEntityType (requiredWhen) -> 1 error; setting notificationType=new_business while omitting notificationEmail (requiredWhen) -> 1 error; setting signatureDate earlier than establishmentDate (crossFieldValidation) -> 1 error. The checker script additionally walks every requiredWhen/crossFieldValidation field reference in the document and confirms each resolves to a real fields[] entry (0 dangling references). This document has 0 documents[] entries, so no documents[]-requiredness path applies (see Scope above for why). Validation: node tools/validate.mjs and node tools/validate-ajv.mjs both pass individually and as part of a full-registry run from a clean checkout of this branch; node tools/verify-sources.mjs confirms this document's own source.url resolves.

fields.name: signatureArrangement
fields.label: Signature right arrangement
fields.description: Which of the form's own standard alternatives has been granted the right to sign on behalf of the business. Make sure the tick is consistent with any articles of association/partnership agreement. If several people have signature rights, it must be clear whether they hold the right separately or jointly; no further restriction can be registered.
fields.sourceRef: BR-1010BM ("Samordnet registermelding", Brønnøysundregistrene, edition BR – 1010B - 2026), page 5/6: "20. Signatur — Oppgi hvem som har fått tildelt signatur. Du kan benytte standardalternativene nedenfor."

fields.name: otherSignatureProvision
fields.label: Other signature provision
fields.description: A free-text signature-right arrangement, if none of the standard alternatives applies. If signature rights are assigned to natural persons or businesses, name, address, and national identity number/D number (11 digits) or organisation number must be given.
fields.sourceRef: BR-1010BM ("Samordnet registermelding", Brønnøysundregistrene, edition BR – 1010B - 2026), page 5/6: "Annen signaturbestemmelse:"

fields.name: procuraArrangement

fields.sourceRef: BR-1010BM ("Samordnet registermelding", Brønnøysundregistrene, edition BR – 1010B - 2026), page 5/6: "21. Prokura — Standardalternativene nedenfor kan du bruke dersom det er tildelt prokura."

fields.name: otherProcuraProvision
fields.label: Other procuration provision
fields.description: A free-text procuration arrangement, if neither standard alternative applies. If procuration is granted to natural persons, name, address, and national identity number/D number (11 digits) must be given.
fields.sourceRef: BR-1010BM ("Samordnet registermelding", Brønnøysundregistrene, edition BR – 1010B - 2026), page 5/6: "Annen prokurabestemmelse:"

fields.description: The business opts out of auditing its accounts. See §7-6 of the Companies Act (aksjeloven) for the rules on opting out of an audit for a private limited company.

fields.name: otherRemarks
fields.label: Other remarks/information
fields.description: Free-text field for information not captured elsewhere, e.g. a power of attorney to the board, a convertible loan, issuance of subscription-right shares/free subscription rights, a notifiable agreement with shareholders or members of the company's management, self-resignation, or a request to be registered under the organisation form PERS (personal employer) or in the Register of Political Parties.

fields.name: notificationEmail

fields.name: notificationMobileNumber

fields.name: signatureDate
fields.label: Date of signature
fields.description: The date the notification is signed. We confirm that the reported information is correct.
fields.sourceRef: BR-1010BM ("Samordnet registermelding", Brønnøysundregistrene, edition BR – 1010B - 2026), page 6/6: "28. Underskrifter — Vi bekrefter at de meldte opplysningene er riktige. Dato År"

fields.name: signatoryPrintedName1
fields.label: Signatory 1: printed name (block letters)
fields.description: Printed name (in block letters) of the first person signing the notification. The physical ink signature itself has no corresponding fillable widget on this specimen (the form must be printed and hand-signed; an original signature is required in all cases, a copy or digital signature is not accepted) — only the printed-name repetition is modelled here. This schema flattens the source form's own printed 12-slot signatory block into 12 numbered slots, matching this registry's bounded-repeating-group convention. Who must sign depends on which register(s) the notification is for and the business's own legal entity type — see the form's own lengthy instructions, not reproduced as schema logic here.
fields.sourceRef: BR-1010BM ("Samordnet registermelding", Brønnøysundregistrene, edition BR – 1010B - 2026), page 6/6: "Underskrift Gjenta med blokkbokstaver (slot 1)"
